overview: Jeskai Control in Legacy leverages white, blue, and red to dominate games through superior card advantage and board control. By smoothing early turns with Ponder, you consistently find answers to stabilize against aggressive strategies. Once stabilized, Jace, the Mind Sculptor and Flow State provide overwhelming tempo and resource generation to outlast opponents.
win_conditions: Jace, the Mind Sculptor token generation and board control
Flow State infinite mana and card draw loops
Outlasting opponents with superior card advantage

keep_criteria: Hands with Ponder or early interaction
Two-land hands with a signature card
Hands containing Jace or Flow State with adequate mana
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing basics for shock lands), Ponder, Jace, or early interaction (Lightning Helix, Path to Exile, Spell Snare).
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking Ponder or early removal
Three-land hands without a win condition
Hands with only lands and high-cost cards
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no spells, 0-1 lands with spells, or hands lacking early answers to aggro/combo threats.

early_game: Prioritize casting Ponder to find answers
Play lands and hold up mana for interaction
Use early removal to stabilize against aggro
Turn 1: Cast Ponder if possible to dig for lands or interaction, then play a land.
Turn 2: Play a land, then cast Jace, Flow State, or interaction depending on board state.
Sequence lands to maximize shock effects and Flow State triggers.
mid_game: Deploy Jace, the Mind Sculptor to generate tokens and control the board
Cast Flow State to accelerate resources and draw cards
Maintain card advantage by trading efficiently
Use Ponder to filter for answers or threats
never cast it when you can't afford to mulligan or when mana is critical.
Manage shock lands to preserve life total and enable Flow State.
Hold back on committing Jace or Flow State if the opponent has sweepers or targeted removal.
Commit threats when you have card advantage, opponent is tapped out, or you can protect your board.
late_game: Leverage Jace's token army to overwhelm opponents
Use Flow State to draw into game-ending threats
Control the board until you can close out the game
Jace’s +1 loyalty to draw into game-winning cards, Flow State’s card draw and combat damage, or Anger of the Gods for board wipes.
Secure victory by out-resourcing or closing with a protected threat.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, opponent has no sweepers, or you can protect your finisher.
Cast Flow State when you can attack safely or when you need to close out the game.

key_cards.name: Jace, the Mind Sculptor
key_cards.notes: Use Memory Drain to strip opponent's hand, then generate 1/1 blue tokens to block and attack. Protect with counterspells.

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Cast when you control multiple permanents to draw cards and untap lands, enabling massive turns and finding answers.

key_cards.name: Ponder
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn one to find lands or key answers. Always put the worst card on top to maintain consistency.

sequencing_tips: Cast Ponder before committing to a plan to ensure you have the right answers
Hold up blue for Jace's memory drain before attacking
Use Flow State after playing permanents to maximize card draw and land untaps
Prioritize countering opponent's key threats over drawing cards early
Ponder is the deck’s backbone—cast it aggressively to find answers or threats, but avoid it when you’re at 1 life or need to protect a board.
Flow State is a double-edged sword
only cast it when you can protect it or when you’re ready to attack.
protect him with removal or by playing around sweepers.
Never overextend into known sweepers, and always track the opponent’s graveyard and mana.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early removal and Ponder to find answers. Hold up mana for interaction and stabilize with Jace.
Use Ponder to find counterspells. Disrupt their setup early and use Jace to strip their hand.
Race with Jace and Flow State. Trade efficiently and use card advantage to outlast.
Dig through counters with Ponder, cast spells in response to counterspells, or sideboard in uncounterable threats. Use Flow State to out-value and pressure.
Apply early pressure with interaction, use Surgical Extraction/Rest in Peace to disrupt, and leverage Jace to dig through their threats. Flow State can out-draw their ramp.

Win through card advantage and sideboard positioning. Prioritize Ponder, Jace, and Flow State. Bring in Anger of the Gods, Surgical Extraction, and Rest in Peace to disrupt their game plan and protect yours.

common_threats: Watch for Tron’s land count, Sneak & Show’s graveyard, Delver’s flip progress, and Dimir’s mana holding.
Key tells include opponent tapping for multiple colors, holding up mana on your turn, or graveyard interactions.
